smedDesc: A systematic method for reducing changeover time between product runs, converting internal setup tasks to external ones.
tpmDesc: A holistic maintenance strategy aiming for zero breakdowns, zero defects, and zero accidents through operator-driven autonomous maintenance.
oeeDesc: The gold-standard metric combining Availability, Performance, and Quality to measure how effectively manufacturing equipment is utilized.
sqcdpDesc: A structured daily management board used in tier meetings to review performance across all five key operational dimensions.
vsmDesc: A Lean method for visualizing the entire flow of materials and information from raw material to customer delivery.
ciltDesc: Operator-level autonomous maintenance checklist forming the foundation of TPM, catching equipment deterioration early.
lswDesc: Defined recurring tasks and behaviors for each leadership level, ensuring leaders spend time on high-value activities instead of firefighting.
ncrDesc: A formal document recording any product, process, or system deviation from specified requirements, triggering investigation and corrective action.
capaDesc: A systematic process to investigate root causes of non-conformances (corrective) and prevent potential problems before they occur (preventive).
spcDesc: A method using control charts to monitor process variation over time, distinguishing normal variation from abnormal signals requiring action.
dmaicDesc: The five-phase Six Sigma improvement methodology that provides a structured approach to solving complex problems.
timwoodsFull: Transport, Inventory, Motion, Waiting, Over-production, Over-processing, Defects, Skills
timwoodsDesc: The eight types of manufacturing waste identified in Lean. Eliminating these wastes is the core purpose of Lean manufacturing.
fivesFull: Sort, Set in Order, Shine, Standardize, Sustain
fivesDesc: A workplace organization methodology that creates visual order, reduces waste, and establishes the foundation for all other Lean improvements.
sixsFull: Sort, Set in Order, Shine, Standardize, Sustain, Safety
sixsDesc: The 5S methodology extended with Safety as the sixth pillar, emphasizing that workplace organization must always include hazard prevention.
bomDesc: A comprehensive list of raw materials, components, and assemblies required to manufacture a product, including quantities and specifications.
qcDesc: The systematic process of checking products and processes against defined standards to ensure they meet customer requirements.
pdcaDesc: The Deming Cycle — a four-step iterative management method for continuous improvement of processes and products.
gembaDesc: A Japanese term meaning 'the actual place'. In Lean, it refers to going to the shop floor to observe work, understand processes, and identify waste first-hand.
andonDesc: A visual management system that alerts operators and supervisors to quality or process problems, enabling immediate response and support.
kaizenDesc: A philosophy of making small, incremental improvements every day. Everyone from operators to managers contributes improvement ideas.
a3Desc: A structured problem-solving approach that fits on a single A3-sized sheet of paper, following a logical flow from problem identification to countermeasures.
fiveWhyDesc: An iterative interrogative technique that asks 'Why?' five times to peel away layers of symptoms and arrive at the root cause of a problem.
ishikawaDesc: A cause-and-effect diagram organizing potential causes of a problem across categories (6M: Man, Machine, Method, Material, Measurement, Mother Nature).
paretoDesc: A prioritization technique based on the principle that roughly 80% of effects come from 20% of causes. Focus on the vital few, not the trivial many.
pokayokeDesc: A mechanism or device that prevents defects by making it impossible (or immediately obvious) to make an error during a process step.
mudaDesc: Any activity that consumes resources but creates no value for the customer. The seven classic wastes are: Transport, Inventory, Motion, Waiting, Overproduction, Over-processing, and Defects.
muraDesc: Inconsistency or irregularity in workloads and processes. Mura leads to alternating periods of overburden and idle time, causing waste.
muriDesc: Unreasonable stress placed on people or equipment beyond their capacity. Muri leads to breakdowns, defects, and safety risks.
heijunkaDesc: A technique to level the type and quantity of production over a fixed period, reducing batch sizes and smoothing demand fluctuations.
jidokaDesc: A Toyota Production System pillar where machines detect abnormalities and stop automatically, allowing workers to fix problems immediately rather than passing defects downstream.
taktTimeDesc: The rate at which a product must be completed to meet customer demand. Calculated as: Available Production Time / Customer Demand.
cycleTimeDesc: The actual time it takes to complete one unit of work from start to finish, including processing, waiting, and handling.
leadTimeDesc: The total elapsed time from when a customer places an order to when they receive the finished product, including all processing and waiting time.
bottleneckDesc: The step in a process that limits overall throughput because it has the lowest capacity or longest cycle time. Improving the bottleneck improves the whole system.
constraintDesc: Any factor that limits a system from achieving its goal. The Theory of Constraints (TOC) focuses on identifying and eliminating the most significant constraint.
wipFull: Work in Process / Work in Progress
wipDesc: The number of items that have entered a process but have not yet been completed. High WIP increases lead time, hides problems, and consumes capital.
